interactions.summary: Jiang answers questions on Saudi/GCC incentives, Trump's limits, spiritual cohesion, nuclear taboos, the American Technate, parasitic elites, Iranian religion, conscription, and why people beat technology.

speaker_notes.note: Focus refs are primarily Jiang speaking; embedded questions are Jiang reading or paraphrasing midterm/audience prompts unless separately noted.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia and the GCC prioritize defeating Iran first because they imagine later uniting the Muslim world against Israel, while Jiang calls them corrupt American vassals.
Trump is framed as a political genius at base control and power accumulation, not a military or geopolitical genius.
For Jiang, spiritual cohesion matters more than physical cities; destroyed cities can be rebuilt, but a people must believe in something enough to die for it.
Nuclear weapons are unlikely to be used because institutional constraints and taboos restrain presidents even when leaders or movements do not morally fear nukes.
The U.S. Technate is a plan, not a guaranteed success; occupying North America would create insurgencies and conflict.
In an oligarchic, rent-seeking system, working hard and following rules can transfer energy to parasitic elites instead of producing fair advancement.
As the war continues and urban Iran is threatened, Jiang expects Iranian society to become more religious, fanatical, and eschatological.
Technology complements people but cannot replace them; dynamic, creative, willing humans can defeat static and predictable machines.

signature_moments.moment: Cities can be rebuilt in five years; spiritual cohesion is the hard thing.
signature_moments.source_phrase: develop the spiritual cohesion of a people
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It names belief, not infrastructure, as the true strategic resource.

signature_moments.moment: When the game is unfair, hard work becomes energy transferred to a parasitic elite.
signature_moments.source_phrase: transferring your energy to the elite
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the immigration-trap answer its bite.

signature_moments.moment: Technology is static; people are dynamic, creative, spontaneous, and willing to die.
signature_moments.source_phrase: technology, it's static, people are dynamic
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the central anti-drone/anti-technological-war thesis.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The shared belief that makes a people willing to die for something; harder to build than physical infrastructure.
A fair-game work ethic becomes a trap when oligarchic elites extract the worker's energy.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-09 Jiang predicts nuclear weapons probably will not be used and Iran will grow more religious as the war deepens.
